rationale: ADDRESSED. rejectControlBytes (panel.go) now uses unicode.IsControl(r), which per its stdlib source (properties[uint8(r)]&pC for r<=MaxLatin1, else false -- 'all control characters are < MaxLatin1') is the complete, correct C0+DEL+C1 predicate, identically matching report.go's stripControl. Re-ran my round-1 exploit (a slug/--bead/--target carrying a valid-UTF8-encoded U+009B CSI) against a freshly built binary: all three are now cleanly rejected with a guard.NewFailure whose message itself uses %q (control byte escaped, not echoed raw), and no panel.json/directory is created. A clean input still succeeds and now also prints 'panel directory: <dir>' (R6's addition) without interference. The 3 new C1-CSI test cases embed the raw U+009B rune (confirmed via hexdump, not an escape Go would treat differently) in slug/--bead/--target positions; against the old r<0x20||r==0x7f predicate 0x9B (155) satisfies neither branch, so err would be nil and t.Fatalf would fire -- these genuinely pin the regression. interfaces.md's doc-sync accurately describes the new predicate and cites the same precedent.
